---
title: TLS
description: Implementation specification & reference for TLS
---

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
import { Steps } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

GoNetSim can run HTTPS and SMTPS using either:

- A user-provided certificate/key (configured in `https.cert`/`https.key` and `smtps.cert`/`smtps.key`), or
- An auto-generated certificate/key.

## Auto-generated TLS

When `https.cert`/`https.key` (or `smtps.cert`/`smtps.key`) are not set, GoNetSim will use a persisted, auto-generated keypair stored next to the active `config.toml`.

The following files are created in the same directory as `config.toml` if they don't already exist:

- `gonetsim-cert.pem`: server certificate PEM
- `gonetsim-key.pem`: server private key PEM
- `gonetsim-ca.pem`: CA certificate PEM 

On subsequent starts, if `gonetsim-cert.pem` and `gonetsim-key.pem` exist, GoNetSim reuses them. This keeps the server certificate stable across restarts, and is useful for malware which pin certificates.

## Trusting the CA certificate

To avoid TLS warnings in tools/browsers, install the CA certificate (`gonetsim-ca.pem`) into your analysis VM.

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="Linux">
<Steps>
1. Copy `gonetsim-ca.pem` to your local user's CA certificates directory

    ```bash
    sudo cp ~/.config/gonetsim/gonetsim-ca.pem /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/gonetsim-ca.crt
    ```

2. Update the CA certificates

    ```bash
    sudo update-ca-certificates
    ```
</Steps>
</TabItem>

<TabItem label="Windows">
<Steps>
1. Open `certmgr.msc`

2. Import `gonetsim-ca.pem` into **Trusted Root Certification Authorities** (Local Computer or Current User, depending on your tooling)
</Steps>
</TabItem>

<TabItem label="Docker">

The Docker container comes with the CA certificate (`gonetsim-ca.pem`) pre-installed in the system trust store, so no additional steps are needed to trust the certificate.

</TabItem>
</Tabs>

## Regenerating the auto-generated certificates

<Steps>
1. Delete the persisted files next to `config.toml`

   - `gonetsim-cert.pem`
   - `gonetsim-key.pem`
   - `gonetsim-ca.pem`

2. Restart GoNetSim
</Steps>
